steps.title: Identify the structures surrounding the blank
steps.content: To the left of the blank: Flemish (adjective). To the right: known for showing the world in a cordiform projection (participial phrase). The blank itself should contain a noun and a proper name.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
A non-restrictive participial phrase must be preceded by a comma.

The blank should contain cartographer Abraham Ortelius, where the title and name form a single unit (no internal punctuation), followed by a comma before known for showing....

Choices B and D incorrectly punctuate the title and name as an appositive, and choice C omits the comma before the participial phrase.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
